qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe exec() in LocalExecutor enables unauthenticated remote code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies unsafe exec() in LocalExecutor.run_code() (openchatbi/code/local_executor.py) via the run_python_code tool. The advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability in the save_report tool (openchatbi/tool/save_report.py) due to insufficient file_format sanitization. These are different tools, different files, different vulnerability classes (code injection vs path traversal/CWE-22), and different root causes (unsandboxed exec vs inadequate path sanitization).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability in openchatbi/tool/save_report.py where the file_format parameter is insufficiently sanitized (only lstrip('.') is applied), allowing /../../ sequences to write files outside the designated report directory (CWE-22). Finding 'rce-via-unsafe-exec-local-executor' identifies a code injection via unsafe exec() in LocalExecutor.run_code() (openchatbi/code/local_executor.py), which is a different tool, different file, and different vulnerability class (code injection vs path traversal). No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: Advisory targets openchatbi/tool/save_report.py with CWE-22 path traversal via file_format parameter
Finding rce-via-unsafe-exec-local-executor targets openchatbi/code/local_executor.py with code injection via exec()
No finding references save_report.py, the file_format parameter, or path traversal in the report filename construction
